per_question.generated_answer:
The system tracks order status history with the physical table `TBLORDERSTATUSHISTORY` / `tblOrderStatusHistory`, which is the implementation of the business concept `Order status`. Its stated purpose is an audit log of order status changes.

Each status change is stored as one history record with:
`HistoryID` as the auto-increment primary key,
`OrderID` as the foreign key to `VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID`,
`OldStatus` as the previous status value,
`NewStatus` as the new/current status after the change,
`ChangedByUser` as the username that made the change,
`ChangedDate` as the timestamp of the change, and
`ChangeReason` as free-text reason information such as cancellation reasons.

The relationship is that `TBLORDERSTATUSHISTORY.ORDERID` references `VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID`, so history rows are tied back to the corresponding sales order header. The current order header itself also has `strOrderStatus` in `VW_SALESORDERHDR`, while `tblOrderStatusHistory` preserves the sequence of changes over time.

A few handling details are explicitly defined:
`OldStatus` can be `NULL` for the initial status,
`ChangedDate` defaults to `GETDATE()`,
`ChangedByUser` references `User.UserName`,
and `ChangeReason` is a `TEXT` field intended for explanatory notes.

In the migration guidance, `tblOrderStatusHistory` is categorized as â€śSecondary (Can Migrate in Parallel)â€ť and described as the order status audit table.
